test_cases.name: Tạo financial model cơ bản
test_cases.prompt: Tạo một file Excel financial model với: income statement, balance sheet, cash flow statement. Dữ liệu mẫu: revenue=100000, COGS=60000, opex=20000
test_cases.expected_behavior: File Excel có 3 sheets, công thức đúng, không có lỗi #REF!, #DIV/0!, etc.

test_cases.name: Kiểm tra mã màu
test_cases.prompt: Tạo Excel với: ô A1 chứa giá trị input 100 (màu xanh), ô A2 chứa công thức =A1*2 (màu đen)
test_cases.expected_behavior: Màu text đúng: xanh cho input, đen cho công thức

test_cases.name: Tạo bảng tính không lỗi công thức
test_cases.prompt: Tạo file Excel với các công thức: =A1/B1, =SUM(A1:A10), =IF(A1>0,A1,0). Đảm bảo không có lỗi #DIV/0!
test_cases.expected_behavior: Tất cả công thức tính đúng, không có lỗi

test_cases.name: Đọc và xử lý CSV
test_cases.prompt:
Đọc file CSV với dữ liệu: name,age,city
John,25,NYC
Jane,30,LA
Tạo Excel từ dữ liệu này
test_cases.expected_behavior: File Excel chứa đúng dữ liệu từ CSV

test_cases.name: Tạo pivot table
test_cases.prompt:
Tạo Excel với dữ liệu bán hàng: product,quantity,price
A,10,100
B,5,200
A,8,100. Tạo pivot table tính tổng doanh thu theo product
test_cases.expected_behavior: Có pivot table hoặc summary table tính đúng doanh thu

test_cases.name: Tạo chart
test_cases.prompt:
Tạo Excel với dữ liệu: month,revenue
Jan,1000
Feb,1500
Mar,1200. Thêm chart line graph
test_cases.expected_behavior: Chart được tạo và hiển thị đúng dữ liệu

test_cases.name: Xử lý text thành columns
test_cases.prompt:
Tạo Excel từ dữ liệu: FullName
John Doe
Jane Smith. Tách thành 2 cột FirstName, LastName
test_cases.expected_behavior: Dữ liệu được tách đúng thành 2 cột riêng biệt

test_cases.name: Tạo conditional formatting
test_cases.prompt: Tạo Excel với dữ liệu điểm số: 85, 92, 78, 55, 90. Áp dụng conditional formatting: >80=màu xanh, <60=màu đỏ
test_cases.expected_behavior: Conditional formatting được áp dụng đúng

test_cases.name: Tạo file Excel mới hoàn toàn
test_cases.prompt: Tạo file Excel mới với 2 sheets: 'Data' và 'Summary'. Sheet Data chứa bảng dữ liệu, Summary tính tổng
test_cases.expected_behavior: File Excel có 2 sheets, công thức link giữa các sheets hoạt động

test_cases.name: Validation và protection
test_cases.prompt: Tạo Excel với data validation: ô A1 chỉ cho phép nhập số từ 1-100. Thêm comment giải thích
test_cases.expected_behavior: Data validation hoạt động, comment hiển thị

test_cases.name: Simple budget tracker
test_cases.prompt: Create a simple personal monthly budget Excel file with columns: Category, Budget, Actual, Variance. Add 5 categories: Rent=1500, Food=400, Transport=200, Entertainment=150, Savings=300. Variance column should auto-calculate Budget minus Actual.
test_cases.expected_behavior: Excel file with a clean table, Variance formula =B2-C2 applied correctly for all rows, totals row at bottom using SUM

test_cases.name: Simple multiplication table
test_cases.prompt: Create an Excel file with a 10x10 multiplication table. Row 1 and Column A should contain numbers 1-10. Each inner cell should contain a formula that multiplies the corresponding row and column headers.
test_cases.expected_behavior: 10x10 grid with correct multiplication formulas, headers in row 1 and column A, no hardcoded values in inner cells

test_cases.name: Employee salary sheet with basic stats
test_cases.prompt: Create an Excel file for employee salaries with columns: EmployeeID, Name, Department, BaseSalary, Bonus, TotalComp. Add 8 sample employees across 3 departments. TotalComp = BaseSalary + Bonus. Add a summary section with average, max, and min salary per department.
test_cases.expected_behavior: Table with correct TotalComp formula, summary section using AVERAGEIF/MAXIF/MINIF or equivalent, no formula errors

test_cases.name: Ambiguous: make it look professional
test_cases.prompt: Create an Excel sales report that looks professional. Data: Q1=45000, Q2=52000, Q3=48000, Q4=61000. Make it look professional.
test_cases.expected_behavior: Model interprets 'professional' by applying consistent formatting (bold headers, currency format, borders, totals row, a chart), output is visually clean even without explicit instructions

test_cases.name: Ambiguous: add some charts
test_cases.prompt: I have this sales data: Jan 12000, Feb 9500, Mar 14000, Apr 11000, May 16000, Jun 13500. Create an Excel file and add some charts to make it easier to understand.
test_cases.expected_behavior: Model decides appropriate chart type(s) (bar, line, or both), creates them with correct data binding, labels axes, and adds chart titles without crashing on ambiguous 'some charts' phrasing

test_cases.name: Ambiguous: make it useful for management
test_cases.prompt: Create an Excel dashboard for a small retail store. Make it useful for management. Include whatever you think is most important.
test_cases.expected_behavior: Model produces a multi-section workbook with KPI summary, at least one chart, and labeled sections — demonstrates reasonable interpretation of open-ended requirement

test_cases.name: Edge case: division by zero guard
test_cases.prompt: Create an Excel file with a profit margin calculator. Columns: Revenue, Cost, Profit, MarginPct. Add rows where Revenue is 0 for the last entry (to test division by zero). Ensure the MarginPct column never shows #DIV/0! error.
test_cases.expected_behavior: MarginPct uses IFERROR or IF(Revenue=0,...) to avoid #DIV/0! error, all rows display clean output

test_cases.name: Edge case: empty data range in SUM
test_cases.prompt: Create an Excel file with a summary sheet that uses SUM, AVERAGE, COUNT over a data range that is initially empty (no rows of data). Formulas should return 0 or N/A gracefully, not errors.
test_cases.expected_behavior: SUM returns 0, AVERAGE returns error-handled value (e.g., via IFERROR), COUNT returns 0 — no unhandled formula errors on empty range

test_cases.name: Edge case: large dataset simulation
test_cases.prompt: Create an Excel file simulating a large dataset of 500 rows. Each row has: RowID (1-500), RandomScore (use formula like =ROW()*17 MOD 100 to simulate), Category (cycle through A, B, C using MOD). Add a summary sheet with count and average per category.
test_cases.expected_behavior: 500 rows generated using formulas (not hardcoded), summary sheet uses COUNTIF/AVERAGEIF correctly, file opens without errors

test_cases.name: Edge case: special characters in cell values
test_cases.prompt: Create an Excel file with cells containing special characters: ampersands (&), quotes ("), angle brackets (<>), forward slashes (/), and unicode characters (é, ü, 中文). Ensure the file saves and reopens correctly.
test_cases.expected_behavior: All special characters preserved correctly in the .xlsx file, no encoding errors, file is valid

test_cases.name: Edge case: circular reference prevention
test_cases.prompt: Create an Excel file for a loan amortization schedule. Columns: Period, Principal, InterestRate, Payment, Balance. Ensure no circular references exist in the formulas. Period 1-12, Principal=10000, InterestRate=0.05/12.
test_cases.expected_behavior: Amortization schedule with correct non-circular formulas, Balance decreases each period, no circular reference warnings

test_cases.name: Sequential: create data then add formulas
test_cases.prompt: Step 1: Create an Excel file with raw sales data: ProductID, Units Sold, Unit Price (10 rows of data, no formulas yet). Step 2: Add a Revenue column with formula Units*Price. Step 3: Add a Discount column (10% if Units>50, else 5%) using IF formula. Step 4: Add Net Revenue = Revenue * (1 - Discount). Verify all formula chains are correct.
test_cases.expected_behavior: All 4 steps completed: raw data, Revenue formula, Discount IF formula, Net Revenue formula — entire chain calculates correctly

test_cases.name: Sequential: create data then chart then format
test_cases.prompt: Create an Excel workbook in sequence: (1) Create a 'Data' sheet with monthly website traffic: Jan=12000, Feb=14500, Mar=11000, Apr=16000, May=18000, Jun=15500. (2) Add a line chart on a separate 'Chart' sheet showing the traffic trend. (3) Apply professional formatting to the Data sheet: freeze header row, auto-fit columns, bold headers, add currency/number formatting. (4) Add a total row.
test_cases.expected_behavior: Data sheet, Chart sheet with linked line chart, formatting applied, total row present — all steps complete

test_cases.name: Sequential: build then protect sheet
test_cases.prompt: Build an Excel financial calculator in steps: (1) Create input section with labeled cells for Principal, Rate, Years. (2) Add calculation section that references the inputs (don't hardcode values). (3) Lock the calculation cells so users cannot edit formulas. (4) Leave input cells unlocked. (5) Protect the sheet with password '1234'. Verify protection is applied.
test_cases.expected_behavior: Input cells editable, formula cells locked, sheet protected with password — cell protection correctly separates input from formula areas

test_cases.name: Sequential: import CSV then enrich then export summary
test_cases.prompt: Complete this workflow: (1) Create a CSV dataset in memory: employee_id,name,salary,department for 15 employees across Sales, Engineering, HR. (2) Load it into an Excel 'Raw Data' sheet. (3) Add a computed column 'SalaryBand' using IF: <50000=Low, 50000-80000=Mid, >80000=High. (4) Create a 'Summary' sheet with headcount and avg salary per department. (5) Save as employees_report.xlsx.
test_cases.expected_behavior: Raw Data sheet with SalaryBand formula, Summary sheet with correct aggregation by department, saved as .xlsx

test_cases.name: Sequential: full financial model chain
test_cases.prompt: Build a complete 3-statement financial model step by step: (1) Create 'Assumptions' sheet with key inputs (revenue_growth=10%, gross_margin=40%, tax_rate=25%). (2) Create 'Income Statement' sheet that references Assumptions — Revenue, COGS, Gross Profit, EBITDA, Net Income. (3) Create 'Balance Sheet' that links to Income Statement for retained earnings. (4) Create 'Cash Flow' that derives from Income Statement. (5) Add a 'Dashboard' sheet with key ratios referencing all 3 statements.
test_cases.expected_behavior: 5 linked sheets, all formulas reference Assumptions or other sheets (no hardcoded values), changing Assumptions updates all statements

test_cases.name: Negative: formulas referencing deleted sheet
test_cases.prompt: Create an Excel file with 2 sheets: 'Source' and 'Report'. Report has formulas referencing Source (e.g., =Source!A1). Then delete the Source sheet. Observe the #REF! errors. Now create a recovery script that detects all #REF! errors in the workbook and replaces them with the value 0.
test_cases.expected_behavior: Model correctly identifies and handles #REF! errors after sheet deletion, replacement produces a clean workbook with 0s instead of error cells

test_cases.name: Simple: inventory management table
test_cases.prompt: Create a simple inventory management Excel file with columns: ItemName, SKU, Category, QuantityOnHand, ReorderPoint, NeedsReorder. NeedsReorder should be YES/NO based on whether QuantityOnHand <= ReorderPoint. Add 12 sample items. Add a count at the bottom showing how many items need reordering.
test_cases.expected_behavior: Table with IF formula for NeedsReorder, COUNTIF at bottom for reorder count, 12 rows of valid data

test_cases.name: Simple: grade book with letter grades
test_cases.prompt: Create a gradebook Excel file for a class of 10 students. Columns: StudentName, Quiz1, Quiz2, Midterm, Final, WeightedAverage, LetterGrade. Weights: Quiz1=10%, Quiz2=10%, Midterm=35%, Final=45%. LetterGrade: A>=90, B>=80, C>=70, D>=60, F<60. Use nested IF for letter grades.
test_cases.expected_behavior: WeightedAverage formula uses correct weights, LetterGrade uses nested IF with correct thresholds, all 10 students present

test_cases.name: Ambiguous: make it interactive
test_cases.prompt: Create an Excel sales tracker and make it interactive. Data: 6 months of sales for 3 products. The user wants to be able to 'slice and dice' the data.
test_cases.expected_behavior: Model interprets 'interactive' and 'slice and dice' by adding dropdown filters, named ranges, or a structured table with filter controls — produces a functional file even without exact specification
